scope: Top-priority PHILOMATH ingestion chapters only. Claims are recorded as best-effort reductions from local QA/Open-Brain-derived notes, not from a verified in-session full text.

claims.claim: Digital root reduces any integer to 1-9 and supports addition, multiplication, subtraction in this space.
claims.qa_rewrite: Digital root = projection ρ₉: Z → Z/9Z with representatives {1,...,9}. This IS QA axiom A1 (No-Zero). Ring operations are standard Z/9Z arithmetic.
claims.promotion_test: Trivial: Z/9Z is a well-defined quotient ring. A1 representation {1,...,9} matches exactly.
claims.notes: Grant independently arrived at the same No-Zero convention as Iverson.

claims.claim: Number 9 is the 'zero of D-space' — additive identity and multiplicative absorber.
claims.qa_rewrite: 9 ≡ 0 mod 9. In the {1,...,9} representation, 9 plays the role of the additive identity. a+9 ≡ a, a×9 ≡ 0 ≡ 9 for all a. This is exactly A1.
claims.promotion_test: Direct verification in Z/9Z.
claims.notes: The 'zero that looks like nine' is the defining feature of QA's No-Zero axiom.

claims.claim: Division by 7 produces the septenary cycle [1,4,2,8,5,7].
claims.qa_rewrite: The septenary numbers {1,2,4,5,7,8} = (Z/9Z)*, the unit group of order φ(9)=6. They form the orbit of 1 under ×2, since ord(2 mod 9)=6 and 2 is a primitive root mod 9.
claims.promotion_test: Compute: 2¹≡2, 2²≡4, 2³≡8, 2⁴≡7, 2⁵≡5, 2⁶≡1 mod 9. QED.
claims.notes: Septenary = unit group = Cayley graph of (Z/9Z)* under generator 2.

claims.claim: {3,6,9} is isolated under doubling and divides the circle into three symmetric segments.
claims.qa_rewrite: {3,6,9} = 3Z/9Z = maximal ideal of Z/9Z = kernel of Z/9Z → Z/3Z. Closed under ×2 because 2×3≡6, 2×6≡3, 2×9≡9 mod 9. The three cosets {1,4,7},{2,5,8},{3,6,9} are residue classes mod 3.
claims.promotion_test: Standard ideal theory: 3Z/9Z is the unique maximal ideal in the local ring Z/9Z.
claims.notes: This is the Hensel tower Z/9Z → Z/3Z. The three cosets organize ALL Philomath and QA structure.

claims.claim: Doubling from 1 traces an infinity-shaped path [1,2,4,8,7,5] isolating {3,6,9}.
claims.qa_rewrite: The ×2 map on (Z/9Z)* generates the full unit group in 6 steps (primitive root). The path on the D-circle is the Cayley graph. {3,6,9} is isolated because it is the non-unit ideal.
claims.promotion_test: ord(2 mod 9) = 6 = |(Z/9Z)*|, confirming 2 is a primitive root.
claims.notes: Yin-Yang property: D-sum of even units {2,4,8}=14→5 (odd), D-sum of odd units {1,5,7}=13→4 (even), 4+5=9. Consequence of unit group partition.

claims.claim: Prime numbers >3 occupy exactly 8 of 24 spokes on the icositetragon wheel, forming a Maltese cross.
claims.promotion_test: Standard: p>3 prime → gcd(p,24)=1 → p ∈ (Z/24Z)*. Converse false (spoke membership is necessary, not sufficient).
claims.notes: Promoted. The 8 prime spokes = unit group of the applied QA modulus.

claims.claim: p² ≡ 1 mod 24 for every prime p > 3; all prime-squares reside on spoke 1.
claims.qa_rewrite: STRONGER: a² ≡ 1 mod 24 for ALL a coprime to 24 (not just primes). Carmichael λ(24) = lcm(2,2) = 2, so the exponent of (Z/24Z)* is 2 — every unit squares to identity. This is the 'max Carmichael λ=2 modulus' property from cert [192].
claims.notes: Grant states this for primes only. QA extends to all units — connects to cert [192] dual extremality.

claims.claim: Primes avoid digital roots {3,6,9}; Vedic Square shows ×3, ×6, ×9 always produce D-roots {3,6,9}.
claims.qa_rewrite: Primes >3 are coprime to 9, so ρ₉(p) ∈ (Z/9Z)* = {1,2,4,5,7,8}. The Vedic Square is the Cayley table of (Z/9Z, ×). Row/column 3,6,9 map to the ideal 3Z/9Z, which is absorbing under multiplication.
claims.promotion_test: gcd(p,9)=1 for p>3 prime → p mod 9 ∈ (Z/9Z)*. Vedic Square = standard multiplication table.
claims.notes: Links Ch.1 (Z/9Z) to Ch.2 (Z/24Z) — the unit groups are related via CRT.

claims.claim: Quadrant symmetries on the 24-wheel: N/S and E/W asymmetry in prime moduli behavior.
claims.qa_rewrite: Z/24Z ≅ Z/8Z × Z/3Z (CRT). The quadrant structure reflects this product decomposition. N/S axis = mod-8 component, E/W axis = mod-3 component. Asymmetries arise from the non-isomorphic factor structure.
claims.promotion_test: Verify CRT decomposition and trace the spoke relationships through the isomorphism.
claims.notes: The deepest structural observation in this chapter. Grant sees the phenomenology; QA gives the CRT mechanism.

claims.claim: Q-grid (multiplication table of prime-spoke numbers) serves as a prime sieve.
claims.qa_rewrite: The Q-grid is the Cayley table of (Z/24Z)* restricted to positive representatives. Composites on prime spokes are products of unit-group elements. Correct sieve, not novel (equivalent to trial division restricted to coprime residues).
claims.promotion_test: Multiplicative closure of (Z/24Z)* is standard.
claims.notes: Correct but not a new factorization breakthrough. The efficiency gain is the φ(24)/24 = 1/3 reduction in search space.

claims.claim: Reciprocal 1/p creates repeating decimal with period that relates to p; half-periods sum to all 9s for primes.
claims.qa_rewrite: Period of 1/p in base 10 = ord(10) in (Z/pZ)*. Half-period sums to 10^(k/2)-1 ('all 9s') when 10^(k/2) ≡ -1 mod p, i.e. 10 generates elements of order 2k but not k in (Z/pZ)*. This is a legitimate quadratic residue structure fact.
claims.promotion_test: Standard number theory: ord(10 mod 7) = 6, half-period 142+857=999. Verify for small primes.
claims.notes: The cleanest promotable content in this chapter. Pure (Z/pZ)* structure.

claims.claim: Half-period 9-complement fails when D(x) ∈ {3,6,9} — numbers divisible by 3 have asymmetric reciprocal waves.
claims.qa_rewrite: When 3|x, the period structure of 1/x is governed by ord(10) in (Z/(x/gcd(x,10^k)))* — divisibility by 3 introduces asymmetry because 3 divides the base-10 digit sum cycle. The {3,6,9} condition = x ∈ 3Z/9Z = maximal ideal.
claims.notes: Correct observation. Links to Ch.1 maximal ideal 3Z/9Z.

claims.claim: Particle-number assignment: particles 1-8, antiparticles complement to 9, photon=9; beta decay checks out.
claims.qa_rewrite: Isomorphism between charge conservation (Z-module, 0-centered) and D-space conservation (Z/9Z, 9-centered). The involution a↦9-a maps particle↔antiparticle. Beta decay: D(1)→D(2+3+5)=D(10)=1 ✓. Valid because charge conservation is a Z-module property preserved under any group homomorphism.
claims.promotion_test: Verify β⁻, β⁺, and K-capture with the assignment table. All check out.
claims.notes: Valid isomorphism, not deep. Any abelian group with involution maps to this structure.

claims.claim: 24-cycle periodicity appears in wave interference node fractal structure.
claims.qa_rewrite: If interference nodes are indexed by integers, their mod-24 residue class determines position on the icositetragon. The 24-periodicity is inherited from the wheel structure (Ch.2). The 'wave interference' framing is observer projection — the underlying fact is just mod-24 periodicity of integer-indexed positions.
claims.promotion_test: The mod-24 periodicity is trivially true for any integer-indexed sequence. The physical wave framing is overlay.
claims.notes: Real content = mod-24 structure. Wave/interference language = observer projection (T2).

claims.claim: Φ is the primordial constant from which all others derive, residing at the core of the wave matrix around mirror number 6.
claims.qa_rewrite: CORRECT INTUITION, WRONG LAYER. Φ IS the eigenvalue of the T-operator F=[[0,1],[1,1]] (char poly x²-x-1, roots φ and -1/φ). So Φ is genuinely fundamental to QA dynamics. But Φ is irrational — it enters QA through its algebraic identity (minimal polynomial), not its decimal value. The 'wave matrix of constants' mapping continuous values to positions is a T2 violation if used as causal QA input.
claims.promotion_test: The algebraic claim (Φ = eigenvalue of F) is proved. The numerical coincidences (φ+γ≈12, golden angle→α) are approximate and not promotable.
claims.notes: Grant gets the right answer for the wrong reason. Φ is fundamental to QA, but through algebra (Z[φ]), not numerology.

claims.claim: Constants transformation table: π, e, α, C, γ derived from Φ via simple arithmetic + angular references (360°, 432°).
claims.qa_rewrite: The transformations involve multiplication/division by continuous values (360, 432). These are observer projections (T2). The numerical coincidences are approximate (φ+γ=11.96≠12 exactly). The factor structure of 432=24×18=24×2×9 and 360=24×15 is real QA content (both are multiples of 24), but the constant-derivation claims are not exact.
claims.promotion_test: Would require exact algebraic identities, not approximate decimal coincidences.
claims.notes: Do not promote. The 432/360 factor structure (multiples of 24) is noted but the constant derivation is overlay.

claims.chapter: Reciprocity of Numbers and Prime Factorization
claims.claim: Prime reciprocals come in three flavors: full period (p-1), half period ((p-1)/2), and other divisors of p-1.
claims.qa_rewrite: ord(10 mod p) divides p-1 (Fermat's little theorem). Full period = 10 is a primitive root mod p. Half = ord(10) = (p-1)/2. Other = proper divisor of p-1. The base 10 = 2G_fundamental (Feuerbach anchor).
claims.promotion_test: Standard: ord(10 mod 7) = 6 = 7-1 (full). ord(10 mod 41) = 5 = (41-1)/8 (other).
claims.notes: Clean algebra. Connects to base-10 anchor via 2G_fundamental = 10.

claims.chapter: Reciprocity of Numbers and Prime Factorization
claims.claim: Prime factors of a semiprime appear embedded as digit substrings within its reciprocal decimal expansion (>95% for 24-bit keys).
claims.qa_rewrite: 1/(p₁p₂) has period lcm(ord(10 mod p₁), ord(10 mod p₂)) containing the full CRT structure of Z/(p₁p₂)Z. Factor visibility as digit substrings is a STATISTICAL property of long pseudo-random sequences. The CRT structure is real; the substring claim needs rigorous analysis (could be selection bias).
claims.promotion_test: Would need: (1) formal probability model for substring appearance in periodic sequences, (2) comparison to null rate for non-factor numbers of same digit length, (3) verification beyond 24-bit.
claims.notes: Interesting empirical claim. The CRT interpretation is the QA-native angle. Do not promote without rigorous null model.

claims.chapter: Reciprocity of Numbers and Prime Factorization
claims.claim: Decimal jumping via binary exponents allows navigation within the reciprocal period.
claims.qa_rewrite: Computing 10^(2^k) mod n via repeated squaring. This is standard modular exponentiation — the same algorithm used in RSA and Diffie-Hellman. Not novel but correctly applied.
claims.promotion_test: Standard algorithm.
claims.notes: Correct application of modular arithmetic. Not a new method.

claims.claim: Numbers classified by polarity (odd/even), primality, rationality, and reptend (reciprocal behavior) in a Wheel of Categorization.
claims.qa_rewrite: Polarity = Z/2Z. Primality = standard. Reptend classification by gcd(n,10) structure: terminating decimal iff denominator divides 10^k (factors only 2 and 5). Mixed form (like 1/6=0.1666...) when n has both factors of 10 and coprime factors. Reptend taxonomy: Finite-Finite (2^a×5^b), Finite-Infinite (coprime to 10), mixed.
claims.promotion_test: Terminating decimal theorem is standard number theory.
claims.notes: Valid taxonomy, not deep. Mostly restates Ch.1-2 structure. The one interesting point: 6 = 2×3 has mixed form because it straddles the gcd(n,10) boundary.

claims.claim: Numbers 2 and 3 are anomalous primes that don't fit the 24-wheel prime moduli.
claims.qa_rewrite: 2 and 3 divide 24, so they are NOT in (Z/24Z)* = {1,5,7,11,13,17,19,23}. They are non-units in the applied QA ring. This is structural, not anomalous — they are the FACTORS of the modulus.
claims.notes: Reframe: not 'anomalous' but 'constitutive' — 2 and 3 BUILD the modulus 24 = 2³×3.

claims.claim: Semiprimes admit a geometric interpretation that reveals their factors.
claims.qa_rewrite: Use geometry only as a depiction of already-verified semiprime relations or mirror planes in factor graphs.
claims.promotion_test: Require arithmetic recovery of the factors from the proposed construction.
claims.notes: Admit only as a geometry bridge until recovery is executable.

claims.claim: Numbers descend through recursive root shells or ancestry trees.
claims.qa_rewrite: Translate into recursive tuple trees, modular collapse pathways, or explicitly generated rooted graphs.
claims.promotion_test: Provide a deterministic recursion rule and confirm repeatability on sample inputs.
claims.notes: Useful as recursive language if stripped of mysticism.

claims.claim: Base-12 provides a privileged harmonic framework for cyclic structure.
claims.qa_rewrite: Use base-12 as one convenient cyclic scaffold among several, not as an unquestioned universal base.
claims.promotion_test: Show explicit computational convenience or symmetry advantage over nearby bases.
claims.notes: Good corpus bridge; avoid exclusivity claims.

claims.claim: Musical intervals and sound structure can be represented through integer ratio geometry.
claims.qa_rewrite: Keep only interval-ratio, cycle, and lattice claims that can be represented with exact arithmetic.
claims.promotion_test: Express promoted claims as exact ratios, modular cycles, or reproducible tuning transforms.
claims.notes: Soundest music-side entry point.

claims.claim: Fibonacci digital roots repeat in a 24-fold cycle.
claims.qa_rewrite: π(9) = 24 (Pisano period of Fibonacci sequence mod 9). The digital root cycle length IS the order of the Fibonacci matrix F=[[0,1],[1,1]] in GL₂(Z/9Z).
claims.promotion_test: Computational: ord(F) = 24 in GL₂(Z/9Z). Verified by verify_classification.py V1-V10.
claims.notes: Fully proved. Cert [192] extends to dual extremality of 24.

claims.claim: Exactly four D-circles (Fibonacci, Lucas, Pibonacci, 3-6-9) exist under the by-sum generation rule.
claims.qa_rewrite: F on (Z/9Z)² has exactly 5 orbits: 3 Cosmos (size 24 each, norm pairs {1,8},{4,5},{2,7}) + 1 Satellite (size 8, N≡0 mod 9) + 1 Singularity (size 1, fixed point (0,0)). Philomath conflates Satellite and Singularity into one '3-6-9 circle'.
claims.promotion_test: Computational: enumerate all 81 elements of (Z/9Z)², apply F, verify 5 orbits. verify_classification.py V1-V10.
claims.notes: Correction: 4 circles → 5 orbits. The Satellite (8-cycle) and Singularity (fixed point) are structurally distinct.

claims.claim: The 3-6-9 D-circle transmutes the other three circles into each other (philosopher's stone property).
claims.qa_rewrite: Adding a Satellite orbit element (N≡0 mod 9) to a Cosmos orbit element shifts the norm class. Since the three Cosmos orbits are distinguished by norm pairs {1,8},{4,5},{2,7}, translation by a zero-norm element rotates between them.
claims.promotion_test: Verify: for each Satellite element s and Cosmos element c, the sum s+c lands in a different Cosmos orbit than c.
claims.notes: Algebraic mechanism: zero-norm translation. Elegant QA proof of the Philomath phenomenology.

claims.claim: The diagonal triplet groups {1,4,7}, {2,5,8}, {3,6,9} organize all structure.
claims.qa_rewrite: These are the three cosets of 3Z/9Z in Z/9Z, i.e. residue classes mod 3 within mod 9. The structure is a consequence of 9 = 3² and the Hensel lift from mod-3 to mod-9.
claims.notes: Fully proved. Links to GF(9) / Hensel lift story (see OB entry on GF(9) corrected explanation).

claims.claim: These sequences are 'consciousness numbers' requiring memory/feedback, unlike formula-generated sequences.
claims.qa_rewrite: The Fibonacci recurrence U_{n+1} = U_n + U_{n-1} is a 2nd-order linear recurrence, equivalent to the T-operator F acting on 2D state vectors (U_n, U_{n-1}). 'Needing memory' = 2D state space, which is WHY orbits live on (Z/9Z)² rather than Z/9Z. The eigenvalues of F are φ and -1/φ, connecting to the golden ratio.
claims.promotion_test: Algebraic: characteristic polynomial of F is x²-x-1, roots are φ and -1/φ. The 2D state requirement is intrinsic to the recurrence order.
claims.notes: Rewrite strips mysticism; the structural content (2nd-order recurrence → 2D state → richer orbit structure) is genuine and non-trivial.

claims.claim: Specific integers such as 108, 216, and 432 carry privileged harmonic significance.
claims.qa_rewrite: Downgrade to optional harmonic motifs unless a modular derivation produces them non-arbitrarily.
claims.promotion_test: Require explicit generation rule and comparison against nearby control values.
claims.notes: Original claim retained as overlay. The substantive chapter content is captured in claims 1-5 above.

claims.claim: Time and space can be replaced by tuple-phase and modular displacement dynamics.
claims.qa_rewrite: Treat as a model proposal requiring explicit state variables and update equations.
claims.promotion_test: Produce a discrete dynamics model with observable outputs or certifiable invariants.
claims.notes: No direct promotion without formal model.

claims.claim: Gravity and electromagnetism can be reinterpreted as modular phase-field effects.
claims.qa_rewrite: Keep only as phase-field analogy until there is a precise operator model or empirical witness.
claims.promotion_test: Define operators, compute predictions, and compare against reference cases.
claims.notes: Model layer only.

claims.claim: Consciousness emerges from recursive harmonic self-reference in number-geometry structures.
claims.qa_rewrite: Notebook-level philosophical overlay only.
claims.promotion_test: None until a clear operationalization exists.
claims.notes: Firewalled from validated corpus claims.
